author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
harināma tuyā aneka svarūpa
jaśodā⋅nandana, ānanda⋅vardhana,
nandatanaya rasakūpa

pūtanā⋅ghātana, tṛṇāvartahana,
śakaṭa⋅bhañjana gopāla
muralī⋅vadana, agha⋅baka⋅mardana,
govardhanadhārī rākhāla

keśi⋅mardana, brahma⋅vimohana,
surapati⋅darpa⋅vināśī
ariṣṭa⋅pātana, gopī⋅vimohana,
jāmunapulina⋅vilāsī

rādhikā⋅rañjana, rāsa⋅rasāyana,
rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅kuñjavihārī
rāma⋅kṛṣṇa⋅hari, mādhava⋅narahari,
matsyādi⋅gaṇe avatari

govinda⋅vāmana, śrīmadhusūdana,
jādavacandra⋅vanamālī
kālīya⋅śātana, gokula⋅rañjana,
rādhābhajana⋅sukhaśālī

ityādika nāma, svarūpe prakāma,
bāḍuka mora rati rāge
rūpa⋅svarūpa⋅pada, jāni’ nija⋅sampada,
bhaktivinoda dhari’ māge
en_translation:
Your Holy Name has many manifestations: the son of Yaśodā, the bestower of joy, the son of Nanda, and a wellspring of divine mellows.

He is the killer of Pūtanā, the killer of Tṛṇāvarta, the breaker of the cart demon, and the protector of the cows. With His flute at His lips, He is the subduer of Agha and Baka and the cowherd boy who lifted Govardhana Hill.

He is the subduer of Keśī, the bewilderer of Brahmā, and the destroyer of Indra's pride. He is the killer of Ariṣṭa, the enchanter of the gopīs, and the enjoyer of pastimes on the banks of the Yamunā.

He is the delighter of Rādhā, the elixir of the rāsa dance, and the enjoyer of pastimes in the groves of Rādhā-kuṇḍa. He is Rāma, Kṛṣṇa, and Hari; He is Mādhava and Lord Narasiṁha, incarnating in forms like the fish and others.

He is Govinda, Vāmana, and the slayer of Madhu; He is the moon of the Yadu dynasty and the wearer of a forest garland. He is the subduer of Kāliya and the delighter of Gokula, and He finds happiness in the worship of Rādhā.

May my deep affection and attachment increase for all these Names, which are fully manifested in Their true forms. Bhaktivinoda prays, holding the lotus feet of Rūpa Gosvāmī and Svarūpa Dāmodara as his true treasure.
